$comment: Pinned source set for the from-source Neon build (initiative A / Worktree DB, Phase 1 Task 1). Bumped by hand in lockstep — no upstream tooling. See docker/neon-build/README or the phases-2-5 handover doc for the bump procedure.
_neonTag_notes: release-9129 is neondatabase/neon's storage release train (cut off main, 2025-07-25). Its matched compute release is release-compute-9073 (2025-07-25, same day) on the separate long-lived release-compute branch — but merge-base shows neither tag is an ancestor of the other (parallel release trains, not a single lineage). Verified byte-identical between the two tags: compute/compute-node.Dockerfile, compute/patches/pgvector.patch, vendor/revisions.json, Makefile, postgres.mk, build-tools/Dockerfile, rust-toolchain.toml. Since every file this build recipe reads is identical at both tags, release-9129 alone is a fully coherent single pin — no need to special-case the compute tag.
postgresForkCommit: see majors[*].postgresForkCommit — the neon postgres fork is tracked as 4 independent per-major submodules (vendor/postgres-v14..v17), not one commit. This top-level key is kept for schema compatibility and intentionally non-resolvable; consumers must read the per-major value.

pgvector._notes: NOT a git submodule upstream (unlike neond's own /pgvector submodule wrapper). neondatabase/neon fetches pgvector as a release tarball inside compute/compute-node.Dockerfile's pgvector-src stage, sha256-pinned, then applies compute/patches/pgvector.patch (adds NEON_SMGR unlogged-build hook + a v0.7.4 SQL upgrade path). One pgvector build is shared across all 4 PG majors — pin is per-source, not per-major. commit resolved via `git ls-remote --tags https://github.com/pgvector/pgvector.git v0.8.0` (read-only lookup against pgvector's own repo, not neon's tree).

pgCron._notes: Pinned to the pg_cron source used by the official Neon compute recipe at this engine release. Release 1.6.4 installs SQL extension version 1.6. Built separately against every pg_install/v14..v17 via that installation's pg_config.

sfcgal._notes: Bookworm-compatible SFCGAL dependency pinned by the official Neon PostGIS build recipe. Built once, then used by every per-major PostGIS build.

postgis._notes: Version split follows the official Neon compute images for this source release: 3.3.3 on PostgreSQL 14-16 and 3.5.0 on PostgreSQL 17. Each release is rebuilt against the exact bundled pg_config.

vanillaPostgres._notes: TRUE upstream Postgres (NOT the neon fork) for pg_install/vanilla_v17 — storage_controller's catalog DB (storcon_db) host. The neon-forked v17 emits Neon-custom WAL (rmgr id 134) that FATALs on crash recovery when the neon extension is not loaded during redo — fine for a neon compute, fatal for a standalone catalog after an unclean stop (Phase-1 Task-3 integration finding: storcon_db redo hit 'resource manager with ID 134 not registered'). neond builds vanilla from the upstream postgres/postgres submodule for exactly this reason (Makefile:21-24 vanillapg: ./configure --without-icu --with-openssl). REL_17_5 matches the neon-v17 minor (17.5). commit resolved via `git ls-remote --tags https://github.com/postgres/postgres.git REL_17_5`.

_rust_notes: Upstream's pinned toolchain (neon/rust-toolchain.toml channel + build-tools/Dockerfile RUSTC_VERSION, both confirmed at release-9129). Deliberately NOT neond's drifted rust:1.94.1-bookworm base image.
_debianVersion_notes: compute/compute-node.Dockerfile's own default (ARG DEBIAN_VERSION=bookworm) at release-9129 — passed as a build arg to upstream's unmodified Dockerfile, not a fork.

currentBaselineAtPinTime._source: docker exec docker-devdb-1 (neond-baked image), captured 2026-07-06 for the coherence check — must be <= the majors[*].minor values above.

publishedDigests._notes: Multi-arch (amd64+arm64) manifest-list digests published to GHCR by .github/workflows/build-neon-engine.yml (Phase-2 Task 5 -> recorded here in Task 6). The registry is PRIVATE (namespace ghcr.io/vangoghsoftware; GHCR packages born under a private repo are private, no visibility action taken). Each `manifestDigest` is the digest of the multi-arch MANIFEST LIST that `docker buildx imagetools create` stitched from the per-arch pushes, so `<repo>@<manifestDigest>` pulls the host's arch automatically. docker/Dockerfile's engine FROM pins images.engine.manifestDigest exactly; the runtime dynamic-minor pull (packages/daemon/src/compute/builds/oci.ts) resolves the compute images by tag once the GHCR opt-in env vars are set (DEVDB_PG_REGISTRY_BASE / DEVDB_PG_IMAGE_TEMPLATE / DEVDB_PG_REGISTRY_TOKEN). See docker/BINARIES.md -> Private-registry access. For-reference per-arch ENGINE digests (NOT pinned by anything; the manifest list above is): arm64 sha256:f2bd45f5d8408609799c0bcb09000a9846edb295a008f57fb7b6dbc90a2c1a4a, amd64 sha256:eff59ef2fa03832962f83c9aeee33ff61b7ff21c63a44a59f2ae59a294c24c76.
